name: nu_zeno_van_Hove
app_r_label: Zeno Drive frequency ν_Zeno (magnon-polaron avoided crossing)
formula: ν_vH ≈ Δ_ST/h with operative tryptophan indole band Δ_ST/h ≈ 10–20 MHz (Ch13 §13.1.2b)
tier: Tier 2 mechanism reference + Tier 3 protected-branch value pending O.12/O.23
app_r_precision_str: Mechanism reference: magnon-polaron avoided crossing ν ≈ Δ_ST/h. Operative β-tubulin tryptophan indole band Δ_ST/h ≈ 10–20 MHz. Registered ν_c = 112 ± 10 MHz requires a chiral phonon-polariton enhancement factor of ~6–11× (Open Problems O.12/O.23). The FAD reference gap (30 MHz) would naively imply only ~3.73× and is not operative.

extra.derivation_note: Magnon-polaron avoided crossing: solving v_ph k = Δ_ST/ℏ + Jk² in the small-J regime gives k_c ≈ Δ_ST/(ℏ v_ph), and the avoided-crossing frequency on the lower branch is ν ≈ Δ_ST/h. The operative β-tubulin tryptophan indole hyperfine band is Δ_ST/h ≈ 10–20 MHz (one indole N1 ≈ 14–17 MHz plus weaker ring protons), so the registered 112 ± 10 MHz peak requires a chiral phonon-polariton renormalization of ~6–11× (Open Problems O.12/O.23; analogous to the ~41× coupling renormalization that lifts g_single^bare = 931 kHz to g_single^eff = 38.3 MHz per App Q). The FAD•⁻ semiquinone values (Δ_ST/h ≈ 30 MHz) are a literature order-of-magnitude reference only; naive substitution would understate the burden as ~3.7×.
